title: Unit, Integration, CLI, Fixture, and Changed-Case Testing · Program Brief: Table Cases
summary: Tests assert only one public-domain English sample total, mock every filesystem call, compare unordered dictionaries, share mutable fixtures, and never run the installed command. Predict the user decision, input identity, text policy, expected report, failure boundary, and evidence that could disprove the proposed implementation before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the table cases increment in risk-layered Bookbot test suite. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.
Partition tests across pure units, file integration, CLI behavior, report snapshots, package installation, and stakeholder acceptance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: bookbot-table-cases — Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.
# Evidence: bookbot-worked-bookbot-testing-fixtures-4
# Competency: bookbot-worked-bookbot-testing-fixtures-4.
# Case 1655: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content.
def worked_bookbot_testing_fixtures_4(unit_cases: int = 18, integration_cases: int = 7, cli_cases: int = 8, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, installed_smoke: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "corpusworked4",
        "policy4dekrow",
        "revision1655",
        "commandworked41655",
        "report4dekrow1655",
        "test1655worked4",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, integration_cases, cli_cases) < 1:
        return False, 0, "test-layers"
    if integration_cases < 2:
        return False, integration_cases, "filesystem-evidence"
    if cli_cases < 2:
        return False, cli_cases, "cli-evidence"
    if not changed_defect_detected or not installed_smoke:
        return False, unit_cases, "weak-suite"
    total = unit_cases + integration_cases + cli_cases
    assert total >= 7
    return True, total, "risk-layered-tests"

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-table-cases-ivl5bm
# Competency: bookbot-table-cases.
# Case 1756: a multilingual community archive intake.
# Operating constraint: test empty, malformed, Unicode, tied, large, changed-policy, and rejected cases before accepting output.
# Changed case: change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy.
def evidence_bookbot_table_cases_ivl5bm(unit_cases: int = 18, integration_cases: int = 7, cli_cases: int = 8, changed_defect_detected: bool = True, installed_smoke: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ivl5bm = "ivl5bm-mb5lvi-1756"
    assert evidence_variant_ivl5bm
    evidence_axes_ivl5bm = (
        "corpusivl5bm",
        "policymb5lvi",
        "revision1756",
        "commandivl5bm1756",
        "reportmb5lvi1756",
        "test1756ivl5bm",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ivl5bm)) == 6
    if min(unit_cases, integration_cases, cli_cases) < 1:
        return False, 0, "test-layers"
    if integration_cases < 2:
        return False, integration_cases, "filesystem-evidence"
    if cli_cases < 2:
        return False, cli_cases, "cli-evidence"
    if not changed_defect_detected or not installed_smoke:
        return False, unit_cases, "weak-suite"
    total = unit_cases + integration_cases + cli_cases
    assert total >= 7
    return True, total, "risk-layered-tests"

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.

hints: For Bookbot and Python Defend the Table Cases model · Theory Table Cases, name the stakeholder, corpus authority, revision, path, bytes, encoding, Unicode, token, count, rank, report, command, test, package, release, and transfer boundary, Table Cases precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Bookbot and Python Defend the Table Cases model · Theory Table Cases, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, normalization, tokenization, counting, ranking, formatting, stdout, stderr, status, test, package, failure, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory Bookbot team handles case 3021 during a clean-environment package release. Build deterministic Python evidence to layer unit, real-file integration, CLI, changed-defect, and installed smoke evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects; then move the input through a symlink outside the allowed root and reject it without reading content. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use named table and subtest cases for empty, punctuation, Unicode, malformed, large, tied, and boundary inputs.

For Bookbot and Python Defend the Table Cases model · Theory Table Cases, challenge the documented misconception with changed-file, changed-encoding, Unicode, token, ranking, stream, CLI, test, package, security, accessibility, failure, recovery, and controlled native-transfer evidence: One representative fixture can cover every important text behavior.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: bookbot-table-cases-ivl5bm.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: bookbot-table-cases-ivl5bm(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_bookbot_table_cases_ivl5bm\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?min\(unit_cases)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?integration_cases < 2)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?cli_cases < 2)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not changed_defect_detected)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using named and table plus specific case evidence.
